title: thelypteris lepidula
intro: Thelypteris lepidula, also known as the leathery lip fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Thelypteris lepidula include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties can be utilized through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
